title: OT/ICS Threat Intel - Food & Agriculture
description: Curated OT/ICS cybersecurity advisories, incidents and threat intelligence from primary sources. Editorial summaries by Mike Holcomb in partnership with UtilSec.

_otthreatfeed.usage: Free to reuse with attribution. Credit 'Mike Holcomb & UtilSec' and link back to otthreatfeed.com and to each item's linked primary source. Summaries are editorial paraphrase - verify against the primary source before acting.

items.title: CISA flags the Gardyn IoT Hub at CVSS 10.0 — an exposed master key over fleets of precision-ag grow devices
items.content_text: CISA's most severe fresh advisory for the sector lands on the farm's edge: on 2 July it flagged the Gardyn IoT Hub with a maximum CVSS 10.0 score. The flaws center on an exposed, privileged “iothubowner” key that lets an unauthenticated attacker enumerate every Gardyn device in a fleet, run commands on connected units, and pivot onto the owner's home or facility network; device logs were also left publicly listable in cloud storage. Gardyn's automated indoor-growing systems sit squarely in precision agriculture and controlled-environment farming, and the pattern — cheap, cloud-managed grow-IoT with broad reach — is exactly what is proliferating across the sector. Gardyn says it has fixed the cloud infrastructure and is pushing firmware updates; operators should confirm their devices actually pulled them.

items.title: FortiBleed: ~86,644 Fortinet firewalls and VPNs under active attack — the way ransomware reaches a processing plant
items.content_text: CISA is urging Fortinet operators to act after roughly 86,644 internet-facing FortiGate firewalls and SSL-VPN gateways had their credentials exposed in the FortiBleed leak and put to use in active attacks. For food and beverage processors the exposure is the remote-access layer: these appliances frequently front vendor and operator access into plant, packaging and cold-chain networks, and because credentials leaked rather than a bug being exploited, there is no patch to apply. Valid stolen credentials are precisely how ransomware crews — the sector's dominant threat — get in, so treat any exposed device as compromised and rotate now.

items.title: Food and Ag-ISAC: hundreds of ransomware attacks on the sector, led by Qilin and Akira
items.content_text: The Food and Ag-ISAC's ransomware analysis recorded on the order of 265 attacks against food and agriculture in a single year, with Qilin and Akira responsible for the most intrusions and a handful of groups accounting for nearly half of all sector attacks. Its 2026 outlook expects fragmentation into smaller, specialized crews plus DDoS layering, hypervisor attacks and AI-enabled social engineering. For processors and distributors the practical read is that ransomware — not exotic OT malware — remains the dominant risk, and that segmentation, identity hardening and recoverable backups are what keep a compromise from becoming a production or cold-chain stoppage.

items.title: Gardyn Home Kit advisory updated (Update B) alongside the IoT Hub disclosure
items.content_text: In the same 2 July batch CISA issued an update (Update B) to its Gardyn Home Kit advisory, part of the broader disclosure covering the vendor's consumer and small-scale growing hardware. Operators running Gardyn devices should read the Home Kit advisory together with the IoT Hub advisory, confirm firmware is current, and treat the whole product family as one exposure to remediate rather than isolated devices.

items.title: CISA flags Frangoteam FUXA SCADA/HMI — lightweight HMI shows up on plant floors too
items.content_text: CISA's 30 June advisory for Frangoteam FUXA, an open-source SCADA/HMI platform, is tagged for water and energy but matters to food and beverage as well: free, web-based HMI software is attractive to smaller processors and can end up standing in for supervisory control on packaging and process lines. Review the advisory for affected versions, apply the fixes, and make sure any FUXA instance is off the public internet and behind authenticated, segmented access.

items.title: Delta Electronics DVP12SE PLC advisory — common in packaging and batching skids
items.content_text: CISA's 30 June batch also covered the Delta Electronics DVP12SE programmable logic controller, a compact PLC used across packaging, batching and process skids in food and beverage plants. Confirm whether any DVP12SE units are in your lines, apply the vendor's fixes, and ensure the controllers are segmented away from business and internet-facing networks.

items.title: Australia's enhanced CIRMP rules signal where food-and-grocery cyber obligations are heading
items.content_text: Australia's Cyber and Infrastructure Security Centre unveiled enhanced Critical Infrastructure Risk Management Program rules on 18 June, expanding obligations to address AI, legacy systems, supply-chain and insider risks across critical sectors that include food and grocery under the SOCI regime. Even for operators outside Australia, the direction of travel is instructive: regulators increasingly expect documented, board-level risk management across the whole food supply chain. Track how these obligations, alongside U.S. FDA/USDA and farm-bill attention, may shape requirements for your operations.

items.title: NIST SP 1339 OT Backup Quick Start Guide is timely for cold chain and processing
items.content_text: NIST released an OT Backup Quick Start Guide (SP 1339) on 19 June to help industrial operators build recoverable backups and speed incident recovery. For food and beverage, where a ransomware hit on refrigeration, batching or packaging control can spoil product quickly, tested and isolated backups of process and cold-chain configurations are a food-safety and continuity control. Use it to benchmark your OT backup coverage and recovery timelines.
